name: Barbell Bench Press
resistance_profile: Hardest off the chest (bottom), easing near lockout; moderate lengthened loading of the chest.
rom_notes: Touch the chest with control; a slight arch keeps the shoulders safe without shortening chest ROM excessively.
cues: Retract and depress the shoulder blades
Lower to the lower chest with control
Drive the bar back over the shoulders
execution_steps: Lie back on a flat bench with your eyes under the bar, plant your feet flat, and pinch your shoulder blades back and down.
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width and unrack it to arm's length over your chest.
Lower the bar under control to your lower chest, keeping elbows about 45-75 degrees from your torso.
Touch the chest lightly without bouncing, then press the bar back up and slightly toward your face to lockout.
good_when: You want a primary heavy chest and pressing builder you can progressively overload.
You are training pressing strength alongside size.
You have a spotter or safety pins so you can push close to failure.
bad_when: Cranky shoulders flare up on a fixed bar path (dumbbells or a machine let each arm move more freely).
You are a beginner training alone with no spotter or safety rails.
